title: Equipment Does Not Power On
text: Symptom: Equipment shows no indicators when power switch is engaged. Common causes: (1) Power supply not connected. (2) Blown fuse on the power input circuit. (3) Failed power supply board. Resolution: Check cable connection first. If connected, inspect fuse with a multimeter. If fuse is blown, replace with the specified rating. If fuse is intact, escalate to level-2 support. Do not attempt internal power supply repair without appropriate qualification.

metadata.escalation_level: level-2 if fuse is not the cause

title: Overtemperature Warning Indicator Active
text: Symptom: Equipment displays an overtemperature warning or thermal fault indicator. Common causes: (1) Ventilation obstruction. (2) Ambient temperature above operating range. (3) Fan failure. Resolution: Power off immediately and allow equipment to cool. Check that ventilation vents are unobstructed. Verify ambient temperature is within the specified operating range. Inspect fan for rotation. If fan is not rotating, replace it. If condition recurs after ventilation is confirmed clear, escalate.

metadata.escalation_level: level-2 if recurs after fan replacement

title: Intermittent Operation or Random Resets
text: Symptom: Equipment operates normally but resets or drops function intermittently without a consistent trigger. Common causes: (1) Loose cable connection. (2) Inadequate power supply voltage. (3) Control board fault. Resolution: Check all cable connections and re-seat any that are not firmly seated. Test power supply output with a multimeter if accessible. If voltage is within range and connections are secure, escalate to level-2 support.

metadata.escalation_level: level-2 if connections and voltage check out

title: Mechanical Binding or Stiff Motion
text: Symptom: Mechanical components move with resistance or bind at certain positions in their travel. Common causes: (1) Foreign material obstruction. (2) Worn or dry lubrication on bearing surfaces. (3) Mechanical misalignment. Resolution: Power off and lock out the equipment. Inspect the affected mechanism for visible obstructions and remove carefully. If the mechanism is a lubricated surface, apply the appropriate lubricant per the manufacturer specification (fictional). Do not force the mechanism. If binding persists after clearing obstructions and lubrication, escalate.
